source_test: HEC.FDA.Model/metrics/StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile.cs + Extensions/ConsequenceExtensions.cs (no upstream unit test file exists for this class; oracle-gate-reproduced against the real StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile/AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile/Empirical directly)
note: construct = {consequence_results: [{damage_category, asset_category, impact_area_id, consequence_type, risk_type, empirical: {probabilities, values}, sample_mean}, ...]}, building one AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile per entry (in list order, matching StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile's construction-order iteration) via the (string,string,Empirical,int,ConsequenceType,RiskType) ctor -- the Empirical is built via the two-array (probabilities, values) ctor, then Empirical.SampleMean is force-set to the entry's `sample_mean` field (SampleMean is external bookkeeping, always 0 straight out of that ctor -- see empirical.hpp/aggregated_consequences_by_quantile.json's own note -- so this fixture sets it explicitly to exercise SampleMeanDamage/GetAggregateEmpiricalDistribution().SampleMean with non-trivial, distinguishing values rather than summing zeros), then StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile(List<AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile>). Optional `add_existing`: a second list of the same per-entry shape, each applied via AddExistingConsequenceResultObject(...) IN ORDER after construction -- exercises the dedup-via-GetConsequenceResult control flow (a duplicate (damage_category, asset_category, impact_area_id, consequence_type, risk_type) combo is silently rejected; a novel combo is appended). `method` is `sample_mean_damage` (args [damage_category_or_null, asset_category_or_null, impact_area_id]) -> FilterByCategories(...).Sum(ConsequenceSampleMean), or `get_aggregate_empirical_distribution_sample_mean` (same args) -> FilterByCategories(...).Select(ConsequenceDistribution) stacked via Empirical.StackEmpiricalDistributions(list, Empirical.Sum), then .SampleMean read off the stacked result (the combined SampleMean is force-set onto the refit distribution by StackEmpiricalDistributions itself, left-to-right Sum over the matched entries' own SampleMean fields, in construction/first-seen order). consequence_type defaults Damage and risk_type defaults Total (wildcard) for every entry/query in this fixture, so only the damage_category/asset_category/impact_area_id filter axes are exercised (impact_area_id -999 == wildcard, matching every region). Cases: (1) multi_category_filtered_aggregation -- four entries spanning two damage categories (Residential, Commercial), three asset categories (Structure, Content, Vehicle), and two impact areas (5, 6) for the same (Residential, Structure) combo, to exercise the impact_area_id wildcard-vs-exact-match branch; assertions narrow the filter from damage-category-only down to an exact (damage, asset, area) triple. (2) add_existing_dedup_and_new -- starts from a single entry, attempts to add a DUPLICATE of it (same 5-tuple key, but a different sample_mean, to detect an accidental overwrite) which must be rejected, then adds a genuinely NEW (Commercial, Vehicle) entry which must be appended; assertions confirm the duplicate's sample_mean never entered the aggregate and the new entry's did. All values PIN (gate-captured, never hand-derived) -- SampleMeanDamage/GetAggregateEmpiricalDistribution both route through the real FilterByCategories predicate and, for the latter, the real 2500-step InverseCDF-sampling StackEmpiricalDistributions refit, so even though the SUM itself is simple addition, only the real C# code proves the filter selected the correct subset and StackEmpiricalDistributions' SampleMean bookkeeping combines it correctly.
